page: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, single-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core scores all placing in the top 10% tier. However, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall into the average category. Memory performance is mixed; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average, the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25% tier. Software compilation and ray tracing benchmarks also register in the bottom 25% tier.

Given its high single-core performance and dedicated 4.5 GHz CPU cores, the c8a.large is suited for single-threaded compute tasks, text processing, and lightweight web serving. Its qualitative cost efficiency is defined by its compute-focused resource density, offering high-frequency DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPUs. It is less suited for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, or multi-threaded workloads that exceed its 2-vCPU capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by top 10% scores in single-threaded compression, text processing, and Geekbench single-core benchmarks. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory scores are average to weak, with software compilation and Passmark memory benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the c8a.large highly cost-efficient for single-threaded compute tasks, text processing, and basic web serving, while representing a tradeoff for memory-heavy or highly parallelized workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for text processing and compression.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: This compute optimized platform delivers top tier single core performance and high speed DDR5 memory for dedicated single threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 4.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
Features 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware
Best suited for single-threaded compute, text processing, and web serving
